title: Declared commit and release targets are silent no-ops

evidence.quote: .PHONY: bootstrap clean test lint bench commit release test-with-coverage view-coverage fmt check

reasoning: The Makefile declares commit and release as phony targets but does not define recipes for either target anywhere in the inspected file. In GNU Make, a phony target with no recipe is still considered a valid target, so `make release` or `make commit` exits successfully while doing nothing. That is a release/build hazard because automation or a developer can receive a successful exit status for an operation that was never performed.
reproduction: Run `make -n release`; it exits 0 and reports that nothing needs to be done.
recommendation: Either remove commit and release from .PHONY so unsupported invocations fail, or add real recipes for both targets and list them in help.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests or Makefile smoke checks exercise phony target definitions; Go tests do not validate Make target behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CI smoke test that fails when declared phony targets have no recipe, or specifically asserts that `make -n release` and `make -n commit` produce expected commands or fail if unsupported.
minimumFixScope: Update Makefile target declarations and, if these targets are intended, add the missing commit/release recipes.
